description: In C++, never hand-edit Protobuf/Qt MOC output, template-instantiation stubs, and configure_file headers; change the source and rerun the generator.
body: In C++, Protobuf/Qt MOC output, template-instantiation stubs, and configure_file headers are derived artifacts: editing them directly drifts from the generator and is overwritten on the next run. Use when a diff touches generated files, codegen inputs, or the the compiler, standard library (libstdc++/libc++), and CMake version that produces them. Do not use this to justify patching generated output by hand or skipping regeneration. Actions: locate the generator and its inputs, edit the source, rerun the generation step, then run CMake targets (cmake --build) with the configured generator and CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE so the regenerated output is what ships. Validation signals include a clean regeneration diff, a passing the ctest suite plus a sanitized build of the touched target, and no manual edits left in the generated tree. Risk boundary: escalate before regenerating if the generator is pinned, vendored, or owned by another team, and roll back if regeneration changes unrelated files.
